project_reference.core_research_question: Can we improve injection campaign detection yield and P_det grid resolution through enhanced sampling?
project_reference.current_focus: v1.2.2 Injection Campaign Physics Analysis -- Phase 20 complete (milestone complete)

position.last_activity_desc: Phase 20 verified (7/7 contract targets passed) -- VALD-01 PASS (zero BH discoveries, 916 bins), VALD-02 PASS (alpha_grid = alpha_MC exactly)

intermediate_results: "Without BH mass" posterior peak: h=0.678 (P_det=1 baseline, product-based) / h=0.730 (sum-based)
"With BH mass" posterior peak: h=0.600 (P_det=1 baseline, pre-fix)
**[Phase 15] Post-fix "with BH mass" posterior still monotonically decreasing** -- /(1+z) fix changed scale but not shape
Single-galaxy likelihood peaks at h=0.73 (formula correct in isolation) -- debug session 2026-03-30
BH mass Gaussian index [0] vs [1] has no effect under delta-function approximation -- quick task 260330-twe
Analytic M_z marginalization (commit 15b49a3) did not change peak location
**[Phase 14] /(1+z) at line 646 is SPURIOUS** -- Jacobian absorbed by Gaussian rescaling (Eq. 14.21), verified numerically to rtol=1e-10
**[Phase 14] Denominator (lines 656-685) is correct** -- no changes needed
**[Phase 14] Sky localization weight correctly inside 3D GW Gaussian** -- not a separate factor
**[Phase 14] sigma_Mz -> infinity limiting case recovers d_L-only** -- verified numerically (CV < 3e-10)
open_questions: ~~[v1.2.1] Is the `/(1+z)` at line 679 a double-counted Jacobian?~~ **RESOLVED: YES, spurious (Phase 14)**
~~[v1.2.1] Is the sky localization weight placed correctly?~~ **RESOLVED: YES, inside 3D GW Gaussian (Phase 14)**
~~[v1.2.1] Is the "with BH mass" denominator consistent?~~ **RESOLVED: YES, correct as-is (Phase 14)**
~~[v1.2.1] Does the analytic M_z marginalization introduce Jacobian issues?~~ **RESOLVED: NO, correctly implemented (Phase 14)**
~~[v1.2.1] Does removing /(1+z) shift the posterior toward h=0.678?~~ **RESOLVED: NO -- scale changed but shape unchanged (Phase 15)**
[v1.2.1] p_det in numerator uses detection.M rather than galaxy M*(1+z) at trial z -- documented as known approximation (Phase 15)
[v1.2.1] What causes the remaining "with BH mass" low-h bias after /(1+z) fix? -- Phase 16 scope
[v1.2.2] Are injection parameter distributions consistent with the simulation pipeline?
[v1.2.2] What is the detection yield (detected / total injections)?
[v1.2.2] What fraction of compute is wasted on waveform failures vs undetectable events?
[v1.2.2] Can importance sampling improve P_det grid quality with fewer injections?
[v1.2.2] Does the z > 0.5 cutoff introduce any bias in P_det estimates?

decisions.summary: /(1+z) at line 646 is SPURIOUS -- Jacobian absorbed by Gaussian rescaling (Eq. 14.21)
Denominator (lines 656-685) is correct -- no changes needed
Sky localization weight correctly inside 3D GW Gaussian (not separate factor)
Analytic M_z marginalization correctly implemented (matches Bishop 2006)
/(1+z) removed from line 655 and testing function line 870 -- reference comments added
Denominator confirmed correct per Eq. (14.33), comments only added
MC convergence ~1% relative error at N=10000, acceptable
p_det(detection.M) documented as known approximation, not changed
Quick validation FAIL -- posterior still monotonically decreasing after fix; additional bias sources exist
[v1.2.1]: Keep analytic M_z marginalization (commit 15b49a3) -- correct physics
[v1.2.1]: P_det out of scope -- handled in Phase 11.1
[v1.2.1]: Derive from d_L-only literature + extend to M_z
[v1.2.1]: Use "without BH mass" channel (h=0.678) as reference baseline
[v1.2.2]: Analyze injection physics before next campaign -- GPU time is expensive
All 14 EMRI parameters consistent between injection and simulation (4 intentional differences per D-01/D-04)
dist() defaults identical across pipelines; only h varies intentionally per D-04
d_L round-trip max relative error 2.18e-13 (threshold 1e-4) -- numerically exact
z_cut=0.5 safe for all h in [0.60, 0.90] -- SNR ~ 3.2 at z=0.5 for heaviest EMRI
get_distance() is dead code, never called anywhere in codebase
8 exception types in injection_campaign() traced to few library, fastlisaresponse, SIGALRM timeout
CSV records only successes -- failed events hit continue without recording
injection_campaign() missing AssertionError catch present in data_simulation()
Timeout message says 90s but actual is 30s (_TIMEOUT_S=30)
Detection rate 0.22% -- all detections at z < 0.2, M ~ 10^5.5-10^6
SNR threshold = 15 (from constants.py); 663 detections vs 363 at SNR >= 20 (Phase 17)
Failure rate bracketed at 30%/50% (SLURM logs unavailable for exact measurement)
f_det non-monotonicity at h=0.70 attributed to Poisson noise (within 1-sigma)
z_cut=0.5 safe with >=2.5x margin; all detections below z=0.204
Farr criterion satisfied (N_total/N_det >= 124 for all h)
15x10 grid recommended over 30x20 for ~23k events/h -- 3.2x smaller CIs, 93% reliable bins
h=0.90 needs more injections (47% unreliable bins even in 15x10)
Detection boundary confined to d_L < 1 Gpc, M > 2e5 Msun -- target for importance sampling
Quality flags added as metadata to SimulationDetectionProbability (no interpolation change)
Preserved original np.histogram2d code path when weights=None for bit-for-bit backward compatibility
Used np.digitize + np.add.at for weighted IS accumulation (single pass)
'reliable' mask stays based on integer n_total >= 10, not N_eff
IS estimator backward compatible: max |diff| = 0.0 for all 7 h-values (decisive test)
VRF computed from actual Phase 18 per-bin counts (not generic formulas)
Targeted budget = 70% of pilot per h-value; alpha=0.3 for defensive mixture
VRF 11.8-24.9x for boundary bins across all h-values (contract target >2.0)
CI half-width improvement 3.4-4.6x in boundary bins
Full support proof: q >= 0.3*p > 0; max weight bounded at 1/alpha = 3.33
Boundary condition threshold adjusted from P_det > 0.8 to 'detections in lowest-d_L row' (EMRI max P_det ~ 0.4)
VALD-01 overall PASS: zero BH discoveries across 916 bins, zero monotonicity violations, Farr global pass
Farr per-bin WARN for h >= 0.73 is expected (high-P_det bins); not a failure
alpha_grid = alpha_MC exactly (algebraic identity for unweighted estimator) -- round-trip grid pipeline verified
h=0.70 non-monotonicity in alpha(h) within 1-sigma Poisson noise (WARN, not FAIL)
Quick task 1: Investigate quick SNR check scaling in main.py
decisions.rationale: Ad-hoc investigation — callback *5 is wrong (should be √5), threshold *0.2 is conservative but defensible for chirping EMRIs

blockers: **"With BH mass" posterior still biased low after /(1+z) fix** -- additional bias sources must be investigated (Phase 16, blocked on P_det data)
**Injection CSVs may not yet be rsynced from cluster** -- Phase 18 (yield analysis) requires this data
p_det in numerator uses fixed detection.M rather than galaxy M*(1+z) at trial z -- documented as known approximation (Phase 15)
"With BH mass" denominator uses MC sampling while numerator uses quadrature -- methodology asymmetry documented, mathematically correct (Phase 15)

session.stopped_at: v1.2.2 roadmap created -- ready to plan Phase 17
